headline: London antisemitic attack alarms European intelligence services
description: After a series of antisemitic attacks against buildings and property across Europe, the stabbing of two members of the Jewish community on Wednesday, April 29, in London marks a concerning escalation for European counterterrorism officials.

description: The first international conference dedicated to phasing out fossil fuels concluded Wednesday in Colombia, which hosted 57 countries in Santa Marta.

headline: French economy chills as growth stalls and inflation rises
description: Gross domestic product stagnated in the first quarter due to a decline in household consumption, while inflation rose by more than 2% year-on-year.

headline: At the Musée Picasso Paris, Henry Taylor and the emergence of African American modernity
description: A hundred works by the artist reflect the influences that shaped part of his journey and capture, with intensity, the lives of individuals he encountered as well as the history of an entire country.

headline: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
description: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

headline: Bardella's new economic advisor faces questions over his business track record
description: François Durvye has claimed to have doubled the far-right billionaire Pierre-Edouard Stérin's net worth when he managed his 'family office.' However, several elements have raised doubts over the credibility of his figures, as most of his growth projects never came to fruition.

headline: Were French people told that Chernobyl's cloud stopped at the border? A look back at a the making of a national myth
description: The legend of the radioactive cloud that supposedly never crossed into France has left a lasting mark on the national imagination. Yet that narrative has been rewritten many times over the decades.
